humanities_role_explanation: Designed specifically to evaluate LLMs on culturally relevant Indian humanities content including local history, arts, festivals, literature, and laws
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Text-only benchmark consisting of multiple-choice questions (MCQs) across 11 Indic languages covering both general and culturally specific knowledge
analytical_tasks_explanation: Multiple-choice questions testing factual knowledge and cultural understanding across domains like Arts & Humanities, Social Sciences, Law & Governance, alongside STEM subjects, requiring culturally-aware reasoning in low-resource languages

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Comprehensive benchmark spanning 8 domains and 41 subjects across multiple languages

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluated 45 different LLMs including GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, Gemini-1.5-Pro, Llama-3 series, Gemma-2 series, and various Indic language-specific models

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for their performance on Indic language understanding

arts_humanities_data.note: Arts & Humanities (19.5% of questions), History, Literature and Literary Theory, Language Studies, Music and Performing Arts, Philosophy (Religion and Spirituality)

quantitative_metrics.note: Accuracy scores across different shot settings (0-shot, 1-shot, 5-shot), with performance analysis by domain, language, and model size

english_language.note: Paper written in English, benchmark includes English alongside 10 Indic languages

summary: MILU introduces a comprehensive evaluation benchmark for Large Language Models across 11 Indic languages, spanning 8 domains and 41 subjects with culturally relevant content from Indian regional exams. The benchmark reveals that current LLMs struggle with culturally specific knowledge, with GPT-4o achieving the highest accuracy of 74%.
key_findings: GPT-4o achieved highest performance at 74% average accuracy. Open multilingual models outperformed language-specific fine-tuned models. Models performed significantly better on high-resource languages vs low-resource ones, and struggled most with culturally relevant domains like Arts & Humanities and Law & Governance compared to STEM subjects.
paper_name: Fine-tuning Large Language Models for Automated Pun Translation
